concerns.text: T011-T016 (Spec Alignment) are placed AFTER T005-T009 (Foundational Code/Data Model). T016 (Power Analysis) determines the sample size (N=110 vs N=50). T019 (Data Generation) and T025 (Training) depend on this sample size. Generating data (T019) and defining schemas (T005-T007) before the power analysis (T016) is executed violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule for the sample size parameter. T016 must precede T005-T009 and T019.

T005 (Create data-model.md) and T006-T007 (Generate contracts) are listed as 'Foundational' but depend on the outcome of T016 (Power Analysis) which defines the sample size (N=110). The data model and contracts should reflect the final N=110. Currently, the plan implies generating artifacts for N=50 (per spec) then updating the spec later, creating a semantic dependency violation where the 'Producer' (Power Analysis) runs after the 'Consumer' (Data Model generation).

T012 updates the spec to set convergence threshold to 0.90. T028 (Implementation) relies on a `CONVERGENCE_THRESHOLD` constant. While T012 is in Phase 3, the constant definition in code (likely T004 or T010) should ideally be driven by the spec *before* implementation tasks (T025-T028) begin. More critically, T012 is a documentation update task, but the *value* it sets is a hard dependency for T028. If T012 is delayed or skipped, T028 has no defined input. The dependency chain is weak: Spec Update (T012) -> Code Constant -> Implementation. The tasks should ensure the constant is defined *after* T012 completes, or T012 must be moved before any code implementation that uses the threshold.

T008 and T009 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depend on T005-T007 (Data Model/Contracts) completing first. The note 'T005, T006, T007 must complete before T008 and T009' is correct, but the [P] tag on T008/T009 implies they can run in parallel *with each other* (which is true) but the phase header 'Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)' might mislead a reader into thinking T008/T009 can start immediately with Setup. The dependency note is present, but the visual grouping could be clearer to emphasize the strict serial dependency of the whole block on T005-T007.
